heading: Sanctions for violation of a release condition

children.heading: .—Available Sanctions
children.label: a .—Available Sanctions
children.text: A person who has been released under , and who has violated a condition of his release, is subject to a revocation of release, an order of detention, and a prosecution for contempt of court.section 3142 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: probable cause to believe that the person has committed a Federal, State, or local crime while on release; or

children.label: B
children.text: clear and convincing evidence that the person has violated any other condition of release; and

children.label: A
children.text: based on the factors set forth in , there is no condition or combination of conditions of release that will assure that the person will not flee or pose a danger to the safety of any other person or the community; orsection 3142(g) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the person is unlikely to abide by any condition or combination of conditions of release.

children.heading: .—Prosecution for Contempt
children.label: c .—Prosecution for Contempt
children.text: The judicial officer may commence a prosecution for contempt, under , if the person has violated a condition of release.section 401 of this title
